title: use new containers/common/libimage package
body:
Move all code related to handling container images over to the new
`libimage` package in containers/common.  The new package is an
attempt to consolidate the code across the containers tools under the
github.com/containers umbrella.

The new `libimage` package provides functionality to perform all kinds
of operations for managing images such as local lookups, pushing,
pulling, listing, removing, etc.

The following packages have been moved over to containers/common:

`manifests` -> `common/libimage/manifests`
`pkg/manifests` -> `common/pkg/manifests`
`pkg/supplemented` -> `common/pkg/supplemented`

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
> @vrothberg needs rebase.

Want to get it green first :)  Getting closer.

Finally green. @rhatdan @nalind PTAL.

This is a _gigantic_ change but I did not find a way to split it into reasonable chunks.  Once https://github.com/containers/common/pull/497 is merged, I'll update the PR to vendor the commit from c/common.

I moved as much code as I could find over to use `common/libimage`.  We may find more candidates in the future but I think we're good for now.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Moving back to work-in-progress as I will start tackling Podman now which will likely require some changes to the libimage API.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat rebased and ready for your hungry eyes

buildah is also a library, and this currently changes the signatures of exported functions, renames others, and removes exported types, functions, and packages entirely.  It will break consumers of the library.

> buildah is also a library, and this currently changes the signatures of exported functions, renames others, and removes exported types, functions, and packages entirely. It will break consumers of the library.

Which consumers are you most concerned about?

> > buildah is also a library, and this currently changes the signatures of exported functions, renames others, and removes exported types, functions, and packages entirely. It will break consumers of the library.
> 
> Which consumers are you most concerned about?

The first one that comes to mind is OpenShift's builder, but the concern is a general one that we have as a library.

Yes that is a general issue but that’s an inevitable part of the code
consolidation. We need to do the same with libpod/Podman and eventually
CRI-O.

If changing parts of the external APIs is a deal breaker, I wished to have
known earlier; moving packages and the extend of the changes was discussed
a bunch of times.

@rhatdan, @nalind, I let you folks decide but I may need medication if I
have to thrash this PR.

A possible alternative solution is to cut a release branch now and then
merge the PR targeting a bump to v2.

Let's see how bad the port to openshift builder is going to be.  If there is breakage, we can add back the parts that break as references to containers/libimage,

I looked at openshift/builder which does not build as I removed Push, Pull and changed the signature of util.FindImage.

I will spin up a follow-up PR tomorrow to add Push/Pull back. That's fairly trivial as I can move the logic in `cmd/buildah/....`. I'll also get the signature of FindImage back.
